tool.name: ANGSD
tool.description: Extract Internal Counts for Contamination Analysis
tool.command:
## 1. Link BAM files with proper .bam extensions touch bam.filelist && #for $i, $bam in enumerate($input_bams): ln -s '$bam' 'sample_${i}.bam' && #if $bam.metadata.bam_index: ln -s '$bam.metadata.bam_index' 'sample_${i}.bam.bai' && #else: samtools index 'sample_${i}.bam' && #end if echo 'sample_${i}.bam' >> bam.filelist && #end for
## 2. Run ANGSD angsd -bam bam.filelist -out output -nThreads "\${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1}" -doCounts 1 -iCounts 1 -minMapQ $min_mapq -minQ $min_q -r '$region'

param.name: input_bams
param.label: Input BAM file(s)
param.help: Select one or more coordinate-sorted BAM files.

param.name: min_mapq
param.label: Minimum Mapping Quality
param.help: Discard reads with mapping quality below this threshold.

param.name: min_q
param.label: Minimum Base Quality
param.help: Discard bases with quality below this threshold.

param.name: region
param.label: Region
param.help: Target region for analysis (specify as CHROM:START-END).

validator.message: Region format must be like 'chr' or 'chr:start-end'

tool.help:
ANGSD is a tool for analyzing DNA data, especially when the DNA quality is low, like ancient DNA.
Normal tools try to guess the exact DNA letters (like A or T). But if your data is poor or you don't have many reads, guessing can cause big mistakes. Instead of guessing, ANGSD uses math to calculate the chances of each letter.
This is a quick version of ANGSD wrapped to generate the internal counts (`.icnts.gz`) file required for the ANGSD X-Contamination tool.
**Input Requirements:** * One or more coordinate-sorted and indexed BAM files.
**Output:** * **Internal Counts for Contamination (.icnts.gz):** This is a specific file used for the ANGSD X-Contamination tool.
